text.title: Staging Smoke Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for a per-surface staging smoke checklist a solo dev can actually run before every prod promotion.

rule.statement: The checklist MUST list exactly the top 3 surfaces by user/business impact — no more, no fewer. If the system has fewer than 3 surfaces, list all of them. If it has more, the bottom surfaces are deferred to a deeper regression suite, not the smoke.
rule.rationale: "Smoke" is a 5-minute artefact. Past 3 surfaces, the dev silently skips items, which makes the document worse than no document because it manufactures false confidence.

rule.statement: Every checklist item MUST follow the format `click <named target> → expect <observable verification>`. Items phrased as goals ("verify login works") or as locations alone ("/dashboard") are rejected.
rule.rationale: The click→expect shape is the smallest unit that can be run without re-thinking. Goal-shaped items invite "yeah, looks fine" verifications that miss subtle regressions.

rule.statement: The checklist MUST end with a sign-off line: `Signed: <initials> <UTC timestamp> <staging build SHA>`. The dev MUST physically type these three fields into the file BEFORE running the deploy script for production. Skipping the sign-off blocks the deploy.
rule.rationale: Forcing a typed timestamp + SHA makes the smoke a deliberate gate, not a memory of "I did it". The build SHA also pins the sign-off to the exact artifact promoted, defending against post-deploy regressions on different builds.

rule.statement: The checklist file MUST live in the repo at a stable path (e.g., `ops/staging-smoke.md`) and be committed. It MUST NOT live in a personal notes app, in Notion, or in a Slack message.
rule.rationale: An out-of-repo checklist drifts from the system's reality and is unavailable when a teammate or future-you needs to run it. Repo location means every checkout has the current artifact.

rule.statement: If ANY checklist item fails verification, the dev MUST abort the production promotion, file an issue with the failure details, and only re-run the smoke after the fix is on staging. Promotion-anyway with a "fix-forward" note is forbidden.
rule.rationale: The whole value of the smoke is the binary abort. Allowing a "fix-forward" exception trains the team to ignore smoke fails, which collapses the gate within a few sprints.

example.code:
# Staging Smoke — top 3 surfaces
## Surface 1: Login (web) - click "Login" in nav → expect login form renders - click "Sign in" with valid creds → expect redirect to /dashboard within 2s - click "Logout" → expect return to homepage, session cleared
## Surface 2: Create order (API) - POST /api/orders with valid payload → expect 201 + order_id - GET /api/orders/{id} → expect 200 + status="pending"
## Surface 3: Payment webhook - trigger Stripe test event "payment_succeeded" → expect order status → "paid" - check logs → expect no 5xx errors in last 60s
Signed: RM 2026-05-20T14:12:00Z build:a3f9c1d
example.note: Exactly 3 surfaces. Each item is click→expect. Sign-off includes initials, UTC timestamp, build SHA. Repo-committed.

example.code:
# Smoke notes
- Login should work - Try ordering something - Make sure payments work - Also check the admin panel - Quick look at the reports page
example.note: Five surfaces (over the cap), all goal-shaped, no sign-off. Reading this gives no confidence; running this skips half the items.
